title: Rerank post-fusion by graph priors: liveness, provenance, recency
context: BM25+semantic fusion doesn't prioritize authoritative or current decisions. Superseded decisions can outrank live ones if wording matches better. Runbooks lack intent matching. Touches invariant(s): con_902759b3dc (All JSON writes to .hunch/ must be atomic (temp-file + rename) so an interrupted write can never truncate the index); con_a87360128b (Semantic vectors are a derived layer in SQLite, never the source of truth; reconcile by content hash on reindex); con_e04226bd05 (The MCP server must stay client-agnostic: all assistants point to the same .hunch/ graph; no Claude-only behavior in the server).
decision: Apply bounded multiplicative priors post-fusion to reorder results. Liveness (0.6× dead/superseded, 1.0× live), Provenance (1.0× human_confirmed, 0.85× llm_draft, 0.75× extracted), Recency (0.7+0.3·½^(age/90d)), Runbook triggers (1.5× exact phrase), Topic-chain promotion (inject live topic successors even when only predecessors match).
consequences: Live decisions always rank above their superseded predecessors
Human-authored records outrank LLM-drafted and inferred ones
Runbook trigger phrases beat lexical keyword luck
Topic-chain promotion surfaces current decisions even from single stale matches
Retrieval path remains model-free; priors are symbolic
alternatives_rejected: Status quo (pure BM25+semantic fusion) — ignores domain semantics
Learned-to-rank model — requires training data and model inference in critical path
Bake priors into scoring function — requires reindexing and harder to tune independently
Separate rerank passes per prior — multiple passes less efficient than multiplicative blend

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Status quo (pure BM25+semantic fusion) — ignores domain semantics
Learned-to-rank model — requires training data and model inference in critical path
Bake priors into scoring function — requires reindexing and harder to tune independently
Separate rerank passes per prior — multiple passes less efficient than multiplicative blend
